title: 14-year-old boy faces gun charges after girl shot in Dorchester
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Crime The teen girl shot on Milton Avenue was transported to a local hospital with non-life threatening injuries, police said.
A 14-year-old boy is facing gun charges, including possession of a large capacity feeding device, after a teen girl was shot in Dorchester Wednesday night, police said.
Police responded to reports of a person shot in the area of Milton Avenue around 8 p.

title: Boston restaurant group The Briar Group hit with nearly $400K federal tax lien
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Food News The owner of The Harp, MJ O’Connor’s, and other Greater Boston restaurants says the IRS filing stems from a 2022 paperwork mistake involving vendor tax forms.
Ned Devine's is one of 10 restaurants owned by The Briar Group.
Christina Caturano/Globe Boston-based restaurant group The Briar Group received a federal tax lien notice from the Internal Revenue Services for nearly $400,000 in unpaid taxes, documents show.

title: After SJC arguments, momentum could be building toward legislative audit
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Politics Many steps remain, but justices of the state's highest court appeared ready to break a standstill between Auditor Diana DiZoglio and Attorney General Andrea Campbell.
Assistant Massachusetts Attorney General Anne Sterman makes her case before the Supreme Judicial Court.
Greg Derr/Pool In 2024, nearly 72% of Massachusetts voters approved a ballot question specifying that the state auditor has the authority to audit the Legislature.

title: Wentworth cafeteria shut down after failed inspection
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News City inspectors found widespread rodent droppings throughout the food hall, leading to multiple violations and an immediate closure.
Wentworth Institute of Technology.
(Pat Greenhouse/Globe Staff) The main dining facility at Wentworth Institute of Technology shut down after city inspectors found a widespread rodent infestation, officials said.

title: ‘Aggressive pillow fight’ in Macy’s led to assault at Providence mall, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime The pillow fight gone awry was one of two assaults reported at the mall Sunday evening, Providence police confirmed.
Patrons stroll at the Providence Place mall in January.

Lane Turner/Boston Globe Staff Two teenagers were arrested at the Providence Place mall Sunday after they allegedly assaulted a mother and daughter who told them to stop engaging in an “aggressive pillow fight” in the middle of Macy’s.

title: Readers: Have you ever been to a World Cup game?
author: Autumn Sloboda

title: Is this the best Cape Cod town?
author: Kristi Palma

title: Melrose mourns 9-year-old boy killed by falling tree at school playground
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News “He spent his last moments laughing and playing tag with his little sister and her friend.
” Flowers and notes of sympathy are left outside the playground where a fallen tree struck and killed a young child at the Winthrop Elementary School in Melrose.

Barry Chin/Boston Globe Staff A Melrose boy killed by a falling tree at his elementary school playground Monday is being remembered as a “fun and incredibly loving” child who relished his time on Vermont’s ski slopes.

title: Fundraiser launched for Mass. family who lost a home to fire for second time
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News As of Thursday, the fundraiser has raised more than $8,200.
A house at 25 Birch St.
burned to the ground Monday.

title: Anthony Bourdain’s estate issues statement about ‘Tony’ movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Entertainment "Tony," an upcoming biopic about Anthony Bourdain starring Dominic Sessa, was filmed in Cape Cod and the South Shore in 2025.
